message.content: groom BY; I want your help setting up cerebro to work on this pace in a tmux session that can work when I turn off macos.  you'll need to update centos projects/rbk_alpha* repo view

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Set up cerebro in tmux for CentOS projects

content.text:
I'll start where the salutation asks me to: plainly, where I'm beginning from today.

I'm fresh — I carry nothing from prior sessions. You've asked for two linked things: groom heat **₣BY**, and then set up cerebro so you can work a pace there in a tmux session that survives your Mac going to sleep/off. That second part touches a remote machine and a repo clone I haven't looked at yet, so I'll verify the cerebro state rather than assume it matches what I'd guess. One thing I already want to flag honestly: you wrote `projects/rbk_alpha*` for the cerebro clone, but this station's repo is `rbm_alpha_recipemuster` and the JJK fundus reldir on cerebro is documented as `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`. I'll check what's actually there before touching anything.

Let me open an officium first (it has to land alone before any command that uses it).

Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 68%
Exsanguination: 92 active, 0 reaped
☉260604-1035

content.text: Officium open: `☉260604-1035`. Now grooming heat **₣BY** — pulling the full heat with dockets.

Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 68%
Heat: rbk-08-credential-repairs (₣BY)
Status: racing
Created: 260604


## Paces

### tools-magic-string-sweep (₢BYAAD) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical sweep with per-locale judgment. The grep is the worklist; the rule
table is the decision procedure. Several locales resolve to a recorded "no change."

## Goal
Eliminate `Tools/` magic-string path literals across executable code, replacing
each with the established mechanism for its locale (BCG dispatch vars / tinder
consts for bash; RCG path consts for Rust). Resolve every grep hit — each earns
a verdict, including no-change for sanctioned forms.

## Discovery signature
```
rg -n 'Tools/' -g '!target' -g '!*.d'
```
Key property: every compliant form expands a variable (`${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/...`,
`${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...`, `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...`) and contains NO literal `Tools/`
substring (case-sensitive grep; `TOOLS_DIR` is uppercase-underscore). So the
hit-set IS the worklist: each hit is a candidate violation, a sanctioned
bootstrap, or prose. Triage by locale.

## Scope (subdirectories)
`Tools/` (all kits), `rbmm_moorings/` (launchers, compose, Dockerfile), `Study/`,
`tt/`. Docs are out — see bottom.

## Per-locale rule

| Locale | Rule |
|---|---|
| Dispatched bash CLI / workbench / module | Replace literal with dispatch var: `${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...` (BUK files) or `${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/<kit>/...`. Config path driving a sourcing chain the module owns → tinder const `«PREFIX»_lower_name` instead. Never reconstruct from BASH_SOURCE. (BCG §Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables) |
| Pre-dispatch launcher body (after BURC sourced) | Use `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...` — the `bul_launch` lines already model this. |
| Bootstrap membrane — tabtarget `exec` line; launcher's `source "Tools/buk/bul_launcher.sh"` | No change. Runs before any var exists; cwd=repo-root is trampoline-guaranteed; the single sanctioned literal at the membrane. Launchers are generated by `buut_tabtarget` — fix the generator, never the outputs. |
| Standalone bash not run through dispatch (e.g. uninstaller) | BURD/BURC absent. Resolve own root once via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}` into a local and derive the rest, or document a cwd contract. Per-script judgment. |
| Bash installer/arcanum acting on a target repo (`$target/Tools/...`) | No change where `Tools/...` is the destination contract in the consumer repo — not a self-reference. Verify each: own-location → candidate; target destination → intrinsic. |
| Rust running-code path | Hoist relative to a `pub const {PREFIX}_SCREAMING: &str` (RCG Identity Rule; exemplar already correct: `VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH`), derive every `root.join(...)` from it. No env-var path in Rust — `root` arrives as a parameter. Const placement/name is mount-time; reuse an existing const home if one fits. |
| Rust kit-root literals (`Tools/rbk`, `Tools/buk`) | Same const treatment for completeness; lower risk. |
| Rust comment / docstring | Prose — fix only if the named path is stale. |
| Rust test fixture / expected-output assertion | The spec of emitted output. Reference the shared const the producer uses, or leave as the expected literal. Lowest priority. |
| Config without dispatch context — `*compose.yml` bind mounts, `Cargo.toml` path deps, `Dockerfile` | No variable mechanism. Cargo path deps stay static (no change). compose bind-mounts are relative to the compose file = deployment contract (leave unless an orchestrator var is already in play). Dockerfile `Tools/...` is a comment (fix-if-stale). |

## Out of scope — docs
`*.md`, `*.adoc`, `Memos/`, root `CLAUDE.md`. The acronym maps and spec cross-refs
intentionally register `ACRONYM → Tools/...`; keeping them current is the rename
workflow's job, not this sweep. Flag separately if a rename already left a map stale.

## Done
Every `Tools/` grep hit in scope carries a verdict; candidate violations are
converted to the locale-appropriate mechanism; sanctioned/intrinsic hits are left
with their rationale; lint and the relevant test tier pass.

### kindle-entry-0trick-renames (₢BYAAE) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical rename + source-line fixups per module. Low judgment, except confirming each rbh0 module's gestalt ModuleName (guessed below).

## Goal
Bring all 6 decomposed modules' kindle "main submodule" to the BCG 0-trick entry form `«prefix»0_«ModuleName».sh` (the single inclusion-guard + whole kindle). Every CLI already conforms (`«prefix»0_cli.sh`); only the kindle entry file is non-conforming. Authority: BCG "Module Decomposition" + Fading Memory FM-002.

## Renames
Group 1 — k-form (FM-002 direct):
- rbldk_Kindle.sh → rbld0_Lode.sh
- rbfck_Kindle.sh → rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh
- rbflk_Kindle.sh → rbfl0_FoundryLedger.sh

Group 2 — rbh0 family, b-base → 0_ModuleName (gestalt names GUESSED — confirm at mount):
- rbhob_base.sh → rbho0_Onboarding.sh
- rbhpb_base.sh → rbhp0_Payor.sh
- rbhwb_base.sh → rbhw0_Windows.sh

## Per rename
Rename the file, then `grep` the OLD basename across the tree to find every sourcer and update each `source` line. Sourcers cross modules: `rbfck_Kindle.sh` is sourced by `rbldk_Kindle.sh` as well as its own `rbfc0_cli.sh`, so the grep-for-sourcers step is load-bearing, not a single-line edit.

## Cinched
- CLIs already 0-form — touch them only for the source-line edit.
- rbh0 `0` slot already holds content landings (`rbho0_start_here.sh`, `rbhw0_top.sh`); the renamed kindle entry is an ADDITIONAL 0-file, not a replacement for those.
- No behavior change — pure rename + sourcing-path edits.

## Done
All 6 kindle entries are `«prefix»0_«ModuleName».sh`; every sourcer updated (verified by grepping the old basenames → zero hits); `rbk-claude-acronyms.md` RBLDK/RBFCK/RBFLK + rbh*b entries updated to the new names; the relevant suite is green (dogfight + lode-lifecycle exercise rbld/rbfc/rbfl; handbook display smoke if one exists for rbh0).

File-touch bitmap (x = pace commit touched file):

  1 A lifecycle-split-establish-rekey
  2 B consider-per-person-multirole-rbra
  3 C divest-readflap-poll-debounce

ABC
··x memo-20260604-dogfight-ordain-poll-connect-timeout.md
x·· BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md, RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc, RBSCIP-IamPropagation.adoc, RBSDD-director_divest.adoc, RBSDK-director_invest.adoc, RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc, RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc, bud_dispatch.sh, bul_launcher.sh, but_test.sh, memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races.md, rba_Auth.sh, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbgb_Buckets.sh, rbgd_DepotConstants.sh, rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh, rbgp_Payor.sh, rbk-claude-acronyms.md, rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md, rbndb_base.sh, rbq_Qualify.sh, rbtdgc_consts.rs, rbtdrk_canonical.rs, rbte_engine.sh, rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh, rbz_zipper.sh

Commit swim lanes (x = commit affiliated with pace):

  1 C divest-readflap-poll-debounce
  2 A lifecycle-split-establish-rekey
  3 B consider-per-person-multirole-rbra

123456789abcdefghijklmnopq
········x···x·············  C  2c
·········xxx·xxx··x·xxx·xx  A  12c
·······················x··  B  1c

Every CLI already conforms (`«prefix»0_cli.sh`); only the kindle entry file is non-conforming. Authority: BCG "Module Decomposition" + Fading Memory FM-002.
72	
73	## Renames
74	Group 1 — k-form (FM-002 direct):
75	- rbldk_Kindle.sh → rbld0_Lode.sh
76	- rbfck_Kindle.sh → rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh
77	- rbflk_Kindle.sh → rbfl0_FoundryLedger.sh
78	
79	Group 2 — rbh0 family, b-base → 0_ModuleName (gestalt names GUESSED — confirm at mount):
80	- rbhob_base.sh → rbho0_Onboarding.sh
81	- rbhpb_base.sh → rbhp0_Payor.sh
82	- rbhwb_base.sh → rbhw0_Windows.sh
83	
84	## Per rename
85	Rename the file, then `grep` the OLD basename across the tree to find every sourcer and update each `source` line. Sourcers cross modules: `rbfck_Kindle.sh` is sourced by `rbldk_Kindle.sh` as well as its own `rbfc0_cli.sh`, so the grep-for-sourcers step is load-bearing, not a single-line edit.
86	
87	## Cinched
88	- CLIs already 0-form — touch them only for the source-line edit.
89	- rbh0 `0` slot already holds content landings (`rbho0_start_here.sh`, `rbhw0_top.sh`); the renamed kindle entry is an ADDITIONAL 0-file, not a replacement for those.
90	- No behavior change — pure rename + sourcing-path edits.
91	
92	## Done
93	All 6 kindle entries are `«prefix»0_«ModuleName».sh`; every sourcer updated (verified by grepping the old basenames → zero hits); `rbk-claude-acronyms.md` RBLDK/RBFCK/RBFLK + rbh*b entries updated to the new names; the relevant suite is green (dogfight + lode-lifecycle exercise rbld/rbfc/rbfl; handbook display smoke if one exists for rbh0).
94

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BY

# Paddock: rbk-08-credential-repairs

## Context

(Describe the initiative's background and goals)

## References

(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)


# jjezs_pace ₢BYAAD

## Character
Mechanical sweep with per-locale judgment. The grep is the worklist; the rule
table is the decision procedure. Several locales resolve to a recorded "no change."

## Goal
Eliminate `Tools/` magic-string path literals across executable code, replacing
each with the established mechanism for its locale (BCG dispatch vars / tinder
consts for bash; RCG path consts for Rust). Resolve every grep hit — each earns
a verdict, including no-change for sanctioned forms.

## Discovery signature
```
rg -n 'Tools/' -g '!target' -g '!*.d'
```
Key property: every compliant form expands a variable (`${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/...`,
`${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...`, `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...`) and contains NO literal `Tools/`
substring (case-sensitive grep; `TOOLS_DIR` is uppercase-underscore). So the
hit-set IS the worklist: each hit is a candidate violation, a sanctioned
bootstrap, or prose. Triage by locale.

## Scope (subdirectories)
`Tools/` (all kits), `rbmm_moorings/` (launchers, compose, Dockerfile), `Study/`,
`tt/`. Docs are out — see bottom.

## Per-locale rule

| Locale | Rule |
|---|---|
| Dispatched bash CLI / workbench / module | Replace literal with dispatch var: `${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...` (BUK files) or `${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/<kit>/...`. Config path driving a sourcing chain the module owns → tinder const `«PREFIX»_lower_name` instead. Never reconstruct from BASH_SOURCE. (BCG §Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables) |
| Pre-dispatch launcher body (after BURC sourced) | Use `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...` — the `bul_launch` lines already model this. |
| Bootstrap membrane — tabtarget `exec` line; launcher's `source "Tools/buk/bul_launcher.sh"` | No change. Runs before any var exists; cwd=repo-root is trampoline-guaranteed; the single sanctioned literal at the membrane. Launchers are generated by `buut_tabtarget` — fix the generator, never the outputs. |
| Standalone bash not run through dispatch (e.g. uninstaller) | BURD/BURC absent. Resolve own root once via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}` into a local and derive the rest, or document a cwd contract. Per-script judgment. |
| Bash installer/arcanum acting on a target repo (`$target/Tools/...`) | No change where `Tools/...` is the destination contract in the consumer repo — not a self-reference. Verify each: own-location → candidate; target destination → intrinsic. |
| Rust running-code path | Hoist relative to a `pub const {PREFIX}_SCREAMING: &str` (RCG Identity Rule; exemplar already correct: `VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH`), derive every `root.join(...)` from it. No env-var path in Rust — `root` arrives as a parameter. Const placement/name is mount-time; reuse an existing const home if one fits. |
| Rust kit-root literals (`Tools/rbk`, `Tools/buk`) | Same const treatment for completeness; lower risk. |
| Rust comment / docstring | Prose — fix only if the named path is stale. |
| Rust test fixture / expected-output assertion | The spec of emitted output. Reference the shared const the producer uses, or leave as the expected literal. Lowest priority. |
| Config without dispatch context — `*compose.yml` bind mounts, `Cargo.toml` path deps, `Dockerfile` | No variable mechanism. Cargo path deps stay static (no change). compose bind-mounts are relative to the compose file = deployment contract (leave unless an orchestrator var is already in play). Dockerfile `Tools/...` is a comment (fix-if-stale). |

## Out of scope — docs
`*.md`, `*.adoc`, `Memos/`, root `CLAUDE.md`. The acronym maps and spec cross-refs
intentionally register `ACRONYM → Tools/...`; keeping them current is the rename
workflow's job, not this sweep. Flag separately if a rename already left a map stale.

## Done
Every `Tools/` grep hit in scope carries a verdict; candidate violations are
converted to the locale-appropriate mechanism; sanctioned/intrinsic hits are left
with their rationale; lint and the relevant test tier pass.

# jjezs_pace ₢BYAAE

## Character
Mechanical rename + source-line fixups per module. Low judgment, except confirming each rbh0 module's gestalt ModuleName (guessed below).

## Goal
Bring all 6 decomposed modules' kindle "main submodule" to the BCG 0-trick entry form `«prefix»0_«ModuleName».sh` (the single inclusion-guard + whole kindle). Every CLI already conforms (`«prefix»0_cli.sh`); only the kindle entry file is non-conforming. Authority: BCG "Module Decomposition" + Fading Memory FM-002.

## Renames
Group 1 — k-form (FM-002 direct):
- rbldk_Kindle.sh → rbld0_Lode.sh
- rbfck_Kindle.sh → rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh
- rbflk_Kindle.sh → rbfl0_FoundryLedger.sh

Group 2 — rbh0 family, b-base → 0_ModuleName (gestalt names GUESSED — confirm at mount):
- rbhob_base.sh → rbho0_Onboarding.sh
- rbhpb_base.sh → rbhp0_Payor.sh
- rbhwb_base.sh → rbhw0_Windows.sh

## Per rename
Rename the file, then `grep` the OLD basename across the tree to find every sourcer and update each `source` line. Sourcers cross modules: `rbfck_Kindle.sh` is sourced by `rbldk_Kindle.sh` as well as its own `rbfc0_cli.sh`, so the grep-for-sourcers step is load-bearing, not a single-line edit.

## Cinched
- CLIs already 0-form — touch them only for the source-line edit.
- rbh0 `0` slot already holds content landings (`rbho0_start_here.sh`, `rbhw0_top.sh`); the renamed kindle entry is an ADDITIONAL 0-file, not a replacement for those.
- No behavior change — pure rename + sourcing-path edits.

## Done
All 6 kindle entries are `«prefix»0_«ModuleName».sh`; every sourcer updated (verified by grepping the old basenames → zero hits); `rbk-claude-acronyms.md` RBLDK/RBFCK/RBFLK + rbh*b entries updated to the new names; the relevant suite is green (dogfight + lode-lifecycle exercise rbld/rbfc/rbfl; handbook display smoke if one exists for rbh0).

content.text: Important finding before I touch anything: **cerebro is not simply behind the station — its `main` is at a different commit with heat work the station's recent log doesn't show** (cerebro tops out at `31c3e7c3` with ₣BZ/₣BU commits; the station is at `b9c4753f`). Both clones share `origin` on GitHub. Before I "update the cerebro view" I need to know whether this is a clean fast-forward or a real divergence — clobbering cerebro could destroy committed work, which the commit discipline here forbids. Let me determine the exact relationship (all non-destructive — fetch only updates remote-tracking refs).

input.command: cd /dev/null 2>/dev/null; R=/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster; echo "===fetch cerebro remote (safe, refs only)==="; git -C "$R" fetch cerebro 2>&1 | tail -5; echo "===does station have cerebro HEAD object?==="; git -C "$R" cat-file -t 31c3e7c3d4d4481845c7c900846164b03f320be3 2>&1; echo "===merge-base==="; git -C "$R" merge-base b9c4753fb5d96ef13ec50918568551512774957d 31c3e7c3d4d4481845c7c900846164b03f320be3 2>&1; echo "===commits on cerebro NOT on station==="; git -C "$R" rev-list --count 31c3e7c3d4d4481845c7c900846164b03f320be3 ^b9c4753fb5d96ef13ec50918568551512774957d 2>&1; echo "===commits on station NOT on cerebro==="; git -C "$R" rev-list --count b9c4753fb5d96ef13ec50918568551512774957d ^31c3e7c3d4d4481845c7c900846164b03f320be3 2>&1

content.text:
The picture is now clean and reassuring:

- **merge-base = `31c3e7c3` = cerebro's exact HEAD.** Cerebro's `main` is a strict *ancestor* of the station's.
- Commits on cerebro not on station: **0**. Commits on station not on cerebro: **34**.

So cerebro has **no unique work** — it's simply 34 commits behind. "Updating the cerebro view" is a clean fast-forward with zero clobber risk. (The ₣BZ/₣BU commits I saw there are on the station too, just below its current tip.) Let me confirm how the sync should travel by checking whether the station's HEAD is already on GitHub `origin`, since both clones share it.

input.command: R=/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster; echo "===origin/main (last known to station)==="; git -C "$R" rev-parse origin/main 2>&1; echo "===is station HEAD on origin/main already?==="; git -C "$R" merge-base --is-ancestor b9c4753fb5d96ef13ec50918568551512774957d origin/main 2>&1 && echo "YES: HEAD already pushed to origin" || echo "NO: HEAD not yet on origin/main (would need push)"; echo "===how far is local ahead of last-known origin/main?==="; git -C "$R" rev-list --count b9c4753fb5d96ef13ec50918568551512774957d ^origin/main 2>&1

content.text:
Here's where things stand after the groom and recon. Let me give you the full read, then I have two decisions I genuinely can't make for you.

## Groom: ₣BY — rbk-08-credential-repairs (racing)

Three paces already landed (lifecycle-split, per-person-multirole-rbra, divest-readflap-poll-debounce — per the bitmaps). **Two rough paces remain:**

- **₢BYAAD — tools-magic-string-sweep** — eliminate `Tools/` path literals across executable code (bash → dispatch vars/tinder consts; Rust → path consts). The grep `rg -n 'Tools/' -g '!target' -g '!*.d'` is the worklist; a per-locale rule table is the decision procedure; several hits resolve to a recorded "no change." Ends when every hit carries a verdict, violations are converted, lint + relevant test tier pass.
- **₢BYAAE — kindle-entry-0trick-renames** — rename 6 decomposed modules' kindle entry files to the BCG 0-trick form (`rbldk_Kindle.sh → rbld0_Lode.sh`, etc.), fix every sourcer via grep, update the acronym map. Pure rename + sourcing-path edits, no behavior change.

(The paddock body itself is still template placeholders — empty Context/References.)

## Cerebro recon — with your loose references corrected

| You said | Actual |
|---|---|
| "centos" | **Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS** |
| "projects/rbk_alpha*" | **`~/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`** (rbm, not rbk) |
| repo state | clean tree, on `main`, **34 commits behind** the station, **0 unique commits** — a clean fast-forward, no clobber risk |
| tmux | ✅ installed (3.4), no sessions running |
| **Claude Code** | ❌ **not present — no `node`, no `npm`, no `claude` on cerebro** |

**Sync path:** the station's HEAD (`b9c4753f`) isn't on GitHub `origin` yet (4 commits ahead). There's a direct `cerebro` git remote (station→cerebro over SSH), so I can fast-forward cerebro **without publishing anything to GitHub** — my recommended route.

## The honest blocker

Your phrasing — "work on this pace when I turn off macos" — most naturally means *a Claude Code agent grinding autonomously on cerebro*. But cerebro has no Node, no npm, no Claude Code at all. That path means installing Node + Claude Code and **authenticating it interactively** (your credentials) on cerebro — a real chunk of setup, not a five-minute thing. So before I build anything, two questions:

questions.question: Which work should cerebro be set up for?

options.label: ₢BYAAD magic-string sweep
options.description: The Tools/ path-literal sweep — grep worklist + per-locale rule table, ends with lint + test tier green. More judgment per hit.

options.label: ₢BYAAE kindle renames
options.description: The 6 kindle-entry 0-trick renames + sourcer fixups. More mechanical; touches fewer files but crosses module sourcing chains.

options.label: Both / whole heat ₣BY
options.description: Set cerebro up to work the remaining heat (both rough paces), in pace order.

questions.question: What should actually run inside the persistent tmux session on cerebro?

options.label: Autonomous Claude Code agent
options.description: A Claude Code session works the pace on its own while your Mac is off. REQUIRES installing Node + Claude Code on cerebro and authenticating it interactively (your login/API key) — none of that exists there now.

options.label: Prepared shell for you
options.description: Sync the repo to current HEAD and leave a ready tmux session (repo at the right commit, maybe crucible charged) for YOU to drive over SSH later. No Claude Code install needed.

options.label: Long-running test/verify run
options.description: tmux just runs the pace's verification (lint + relevant test suite) and persists across Mac-off. No agent, no install — but doesn't do the editing work itself.

aiTitle: Set up cerebro in tmux for CentOS projects

content.text: You're right — my probe ran in a non-login shell and missed it. Let me find Claude Code properly (login shell + the usual install locations).

input.command: ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 cerebro 'bash -lc "echo ===login-PATH-claude===; command -v claude; echo ===version===; claude --version 2>&1 | head -1; echo ===node===; command -v node && node --version; echo ===npm===; command -v npm && npm --version"' 2>&1; echo "===common locations==="; ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 cerebro 'ls -la ~/.local/bin/claude ~/.claude/local/claude /usr/local/bin/claude 2>/dev/null; echo "--- nvm/fnm/volta ---"; ls -d ~/.nvm ~/.fnm ~/.volta 2>/dev/null; echo "--- nvm node dirs ---"; ls ~/.nvm/versions/node 2>/dev/null' 2>&1

content.text:
I stand corrected — and I'll own it plainly: **Claude Code 2.1.163 is on cerebro**, at `~/.local/bin/claude`, a *native* install (`~/.local/share/claude/versions/...`), which is exactly why there was no `node`/`npm` and why my non-login `which claude` came up empty. My "hard blocker" was a probing error, not a real gap. The autonomous-agent path is fully open.

That reshapes everything. Let me check the two things that now actually determine the design — whether that cerebro Claude is authenticated, and whether it can reach the JJK tooling (so a cerebro agent could notch/wrap properly) — then I'll give you the revised plan.

message.content: just list commands I run on cerebro to set up the tmux

content.text:
Claude Code is there, authenticated (`~/.claude/.credentials.json` present), and the repo has `.mcp.json` + the `vvx` binary — so a cerebro agent can drive JJK (mount/notch/wrap) just like here. Everything's in place. Here's the command list to run on cerebro.

```bash
# ── on cerebro:  ssh cerebro ───────────────────────────────────────────

# 1. bring the clone current (cerebro is 34 behind; clean fast-forward)
cd ~/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster
git fetch origin
git merge --ff-only origin/main

# 2. start a DETACHED tmux session running Claude Code in the repo
#    (survives your SSH dropping and your Mac sleeping/off)
tmux new-session -d -s rbk08 -c ~/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster \
  'claude --permission-mode acceptEdits'

# 3. attach to hand it the work (and every time you reconnect)
tmux attach -t rbk08
#    …at the Claude prompt, type:   mount ₣BY
#    then pick the pace:  ₢BYAAD (magic-string sweep)  or  ₢BYAAE (kindle renames)

#    detach and leave it running:   Ctrl-b  then  d
```

From your Mac afterward, reattach in one shot: `ssh -t cerebro tmux attach -t rbk08`.

Three honest notes:

- **Sync reach:** step 1 pulls GitHub `origin/main`. That lands cerebro on the real `/workspace`-mint code base — fully coherent for this work. The station's newest **4 commits are officium housekeeping** (not yet on GitHub) and don't affect the pace. If you want them too, say the word and I'll `git push origin main` from the station first.

- **Autonomy level (the "works while Mac is off" part):** `--permission-mode acceptEdits` auto-accepts file edits but still pauses for bash (running the test tier, git). For *fully* unattended grinding — edits, tests, and commits without stopping — swap in `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. That's a real loosening of this repo's discipline; your call which you want, so I left the safer one in the block.

- **Pace still unpicked** — `mount ₣BY` lets the cerebro agent groom and you choose ₢BYAAD vs ₢BYAAE live, so the command list doesn't depend on it.
